assumptions: The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 for foreign currency risk on an intragroup dividend receivable once that receivable is recognised.
The intragroup dividend gives rise to a monetary receivable/payable balance denominated in a foreign currency.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recognised foreign-currency-denominated receivable is a recognised item whose change in value attributable to exchange-rate movements can fall within the fair value hedge model.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS hedge accounting distinguishes a separate top-level model for hedging foreign currency variability in cash flows, which is the peer model to a fair value hedge.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: For foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements, IFRS also treats a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct top-level hedge accounting model.
